title: solenostemon monostachyus subsp. marrubifolius
intro: Solenostemon monostachyus subsp. marrubifolius, also known as Lemon Basil, is a subspecies of plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its distinctive lemon-scented leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Solenostemon monostachyus subsp. marrubifolius include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, essential oil, or dried herb, and is often used in cooking and traditional medicine.
